About

Product Manager with 4+ years of experience in solving complex problems across B2B SaaS, IT and UX products. I did not just work on the surface, but also understood the hidden logic underneath, and that always meant asking why.

My path from bioinformatics to product management wasn't a pivot, but an accumulation. Systems Engineering and Pattern recognition. At Infosys, I was working with cross-functional teams and various stakeholders to maintain the Indian Income Tax Website which sees 80M+ users with around 300M+ records.

I graduated from Duke's MEM program with a much stronger sense of business and users understanding. Now I am ready to take on bigger and more comlpex challenges to make an impact for the end-user.

Product Strategy GTM Design AI/ML Products Stakeholder Management User Experience PLG Strategy

01 — Case Study
ARC Cary — Positioning & Break-Even
Marketing Strategy · Duke MEM
Problem

A wellness brand struggled to resonate with younger athletes, lacking a clear market positioning and a sustainable path to profitability.

Strategy

Developed a phased, influencer-led go-to-market strategy combined with revised pricing tiers and a detailed financial break-even analysis to validate the new model.

Impact

Created a data-backed roadmap that validated the new pricing structure and defined a clear path to break-even — 181 packages — for the business.

Pricing Strategy Influencer GTM Break-Even Analysis Value Proposition

02 — Case Study
Illumina Medical — Strategic Pitch
Investment Strategy · Duke MEM
Problem

Facing a high-stakes, competitive genomic market, the company needed a strategic pitch to differentiate its vision and secure investment.

Strategy

Synthesized complex genomic market trends into a high-growth pitch, focusing on competitive advantages, defensible moats, and long-term strategic positioning.

Impact

Delivered a compelling strategic vision that clarified the market opportunity and laid out a credible, high-impact growth narrative for investors.

Market Sizing Competitive Positioning Genomics Investor Pitch

03 — Case Study
NIKE — Next Phase of Growth
Retail Strategy · Duke MEM
Problem

As a retail giant, NIKE needed to identify its next phase of growth in an increasingly crowded and digital-first market.

Strategy

Deconstructed NIKE's global retail strategy to identify key growth horizons, specifically focusing on digital engagement, DTC expansion, and customer loyalty infrastructure.

Impact

Uncovered actionable opportunities for digital-first engagement that align with the brand's long-term competitive strategy and customer retention goals.

Growth Horizons Digital Strategy Customer Loyalty DTC

04 — Case Study
Sparc — Final GTM Presentation
Product Consulting · Duke DTI
Problem

The platform suffered from stagnant engagement, with users failing to utilize core features — hindering long-term platform adoption and growth.

Strategy

Synthesized 40+ user interviews to identify pain points, prioritized solutions using the MoSCoW framework, and conducted iterative A/B testing across five key features.

Impact

Drove a 16% lift in Daily Active Users and a 40% increase in feature adoption through data-driven product improvements and a focused GTM strategy.

User Research MoSCoW A/B Testing +16% DAU GTM Strategy
Sparc_Final.pdf

05 — Case Study
Warner Bros. — Can They Rewrite the Script on Entertainment?
Competitive Strategy · Duke MEM
Problem

Warner Bros. is drowning in $40B of debt while its biggest franchises are losing relevance with younger audiences and streaming rivals are eating its lunch.

Strategy

Revive iconic IPs with fan collaboration, unlock new revenue through gaming and VR, and use AI to cut production costs, all while making Max streaming actually profitable.

Impact

A clear path to reduce debt, grow revenue beyond traditional film, and position Warner Bros. as an entertainment ecosystem, not just a movie studio.

Scenario Planning IP Strategy Streaming Economics Competitive Analysis

06 — Case Study
Tesla — Competitive Strategy Stress-Test
Competitive Strategy · Duke MEM
Problem

While a market disruptor, Tesla faced increasing pressure to sustain its competitive advantage amidst aggressive vertical integration challenges and rising EV competition.

Strategy

Performed a stress-test analysis of Tesla's strategy, evaluating risks and opportunities across their supply chain, production vertical, and software ecosystem.

Impact

Provided a clear assessment of Tesla's strategic vulnerabilities, offering high-conviction insights for the next phase of its market expansion and competitive defense.

Vertical Integration Supply Chain Analysis Risk Assessment Competitive Strategy
